title: NOC 13201 – Production and Transportation Logistics Coordinators | GTR Immigration
description: Production and Transportation Logistics Coordinators play a vital role in Canada’s manufacturing, construction, and supply-chain industries. Classified under NOC 13201, this occupation continues to experience strong hiring demand across multiple provinces and offers promising immigration opportunities through Express Entry, Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs), and employer-driven work permits.

classification.teer: 3 – Occupations usually require a college diploma or apprenticeship training of less than two years; or more than six months of on-the-job training
classification.major_group: 132 – Transportation and production logistics coordinators and customs and related broker occupations
classification.minor_group: 1320 – Transportation and production logistics coordinators and customs and related broker occupations

common_job_titles: Dispatch logistician Freight forwarding logistician Production coordinator Production scheduler Supply chain coordinator – production Supply control coordinator
duties: Plan and oversee operational logistics within supply-chain units
Coordinate with production supervisors to schedule cost-efficient production runs
Communicate with warehouse, purchasing, construction, and production teams
Coordinate the movement of goods within facilities
Prepare customs documents for international shipments
Liaise with carriers and freight partners
Ensure materials and products arrive on time
employment_requirements: Post-secondary education (less than two years) in Business, Logistics, or Supply Chain Management
Extensive experience as a dispatcher, scheduling clerk, or production clerk may substitute for formal education
Experience with logistics or inventory software
job_outlook: From 2022 to 2031, Canada expects 18,800 new jobs for Production and Transportation Logistics Coordinators, compared with 17,200 new job seekers—making this a favourable occupation for overseas professionals planning long-term settlement.
additional_information: Production and Transportation Logistics Coordinators are in demand across:
Manufacturing plantsConstruction firmsDistribution centersWarehousing operationsFreight forwarding companies
Why this occupation stands out for immigrants:

Growing supply-chain and infrastructure investmentsStable hiring post-pandemicEligibility for Express Entry and PNP programsCompetitive wagesStrong provincial opportunities in Alberta, Ontario, British Columbia, and Saskatchewan
Typical annual earnings range between $34,700 and $83,300 CAD, depending on experience and location.
Why Choose Canada in This Occupation?
Immigrating as a Production or Transportation Logistics Coordinator offers:

Universal public healthcareSafe working conditions and labour protectionsClear PR transition optionsMulticultural cities and industrial hubsWork-life balanceFamily sponsorship opportunities after PR approval
